judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports correctly diagnose all four points and hit every expectation; they are comparable, with Report 1 marginally more thorough in evidence.

report_1.evidence: The root cause is a stale config key in `ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`. Its `expected_label_field` is set to `label`, but the eval-set schema was migrated so that every record in `ticket-scorer/fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` now stores its gold answer under `gold_label`, not `label`.

report_2.evidence: The config declares `expected_label_field: label` (`ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml:2`), but every record in the eval set now stores its gold label under the key `gold_label`, not `label` ... `gold = record.get(gold_field)  # record.get("label") -> None, always`

report_1.evidence: The regression began on **2026-06-17**, introduced by commit `53551e8` ("chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)") ... `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` shows accuracy at 0.91 on 2026-06-16 and exactly 0.00 on every date from 2026-06-17 onward

report_2.evidence: Commit `53551e8` ("chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)"), authored 2026-06-17 ... renamed the label key in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` from `label` to `gold_label` and updated `pipeline/score_batch.py`'s `REQUIRED_KEYS` ... but left `config/scoring_config.yaml`'s `expected_label_field: label` unchanged. `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` shows accuracy at 0.91 on 2026-06-16 and 0.00 starting exactly 2026-06-17

report_1.evidence: `score_one.py` (used for all 20 spot-checks) only calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` and prints the label — it never reads `config/scoring_config.yaml`, never computes `expected_label_field`, and never compares against any gold value ... Correct reproduction: run the actual nightly batch entry point, `pipeline/score_batch.py`

report_2.evidence: it re-ran tickets through `pipeline/score_one.py`, which ... never loads `config/scoring_config.yaml`, never reads `expected_label_field`, and never compares against any gold label. It is structurally incapable of exercising the code path where the bug lives ... A correct reproduction must exercise the actual nightly path, `pipeline/score_batch.py::score()`

report_1.evidence: This would not fix anything and shouldn't be acted on ... because it changes nothing relevant to the bug — accuracy would stay at 0.00 afterward, likely reinforcing the incorrect "flaky model" narrative

report_2.evidence: Rolling back "the model" changes nothing about the `expected_label_field`/`gold_label` mismatch that actually causes the 0.0 score, so the team would burn a deploy/rollback cycle, very likely still observe 0.0 accuracy afterward (since the bug is entirely in the grading code path, untouched by which model/version is "active")
